scope_boundary: Local deterministic public-safe task-bundle accounting only. The bundle compares an autoregressive reference, a verified fast-template route, and a cheaper latency-only proxy over four receipt tasks using deterministic cost units; it does not run an AI model, reproduce a fast-generation paper result, measure wall-clock speedup, or validate deployed routing.
transition_reason: The task bundle is a useful Fast Generation accounting artifact because the verified fast-template route passes the same four deterministic receipt tasks as the autoregressive reference at lower deterministic cost units, while the cheaper latency-only proxy is rejected for missing verifier, fallback, residual, and support-state boundaries. It remains local, synthetic, and record-level, so it blocks promotion for model-speed, useful-solution-per-second model performance, serving throughput, route-selector adequacy, model quality, benchmark, deployed-routing, or chapter-core claims.
required_artifacts: matched public-safe model or decoder run with accepted-output and task-success measurements
autoregressive baseline with environment and workload notes
speculative, MTP, diffusion, KV-cache, or serving benchmark reproduction where relevant
fallback execution evidence and repair/residual accounting
negative_results: latency-only proxy is cheaper but rejected
non-claims reject model-speed and useful-solution-per-second model claims
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: selected route no longer passes every deterministic task
deterministic cost-unit accounting described as model-speed or serving-throughput evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed Fast Generation claim after a public-safe model or decoder run, matched autoregressive baseline, accepted-output and task-success measurements, verifier/fallback/residual accounting, relevant benchmark or serving-memory evidence, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
limitations: no speculative decoding, MTP, diffusion, KV-cache, or serving benchmark reproduction
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent model-evaluation, decoding, serving, benchmark, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no public-safe model or decoder run
non_claims: does not promote Fast Generation above argument
does not prove model generation speed, useful-solution-per-second model performance, serving throughput, route-selector adequacy, benchmark performance, model quality, or deployed routing
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote the Fast Generation chapter core claim
